inkaR 0.6.6
  • Reference
    • Introduction to inkaR

Plot Time Series Trends for INKAR Indicators

Source: R/download.R
inkar_trends.Rd

Creates a ggplot2 line chart showing how indicator values change over time for selected regions. Input must be a long-format data frame from get_inkar_data().

Usage

inkar_trends(data, regions = NULL, title = NULL, mode = c("light", "dark"))

Arguments

data

A long-format data frame from get_inkar_data().

regions

Optional character vector. Region names (partial match) to include. If NULL, all regions are plotted.

title

Optional character. Custom plot title. Defaults to indicator name.

mode

Character. "light" (default) or "dark" theme.

Value

A ggplot2 object.

Examples

# \donttest{
  df <- try(get_inkar_data("011", level = "KRE", lang = "en"))
#> Using M_ID '11' directly.
#> ✔ Downloaded: Gross domestic product in 1000 euros [thousand euros]
#>   Level: Kreise | Regions: 400 | Year(s): 1995–2022 | Rows: 11200
  if (is.data.frame(df)) {
    inkar_trends(df, regions = c("Berlin", "Hamburg", "München"))
  }

# }
